body:
## Resolution

Arbiter decision on all three open questions:

### Q1 — type_id convention: **rge-data scene-envelope strings**

The simple-scene golden fixture will use:

- `"rge::components::Transform"`
- `"rge::components::Camera"`
- `"rge::components::Light"`
- `"rge::components::Visibility"`

**These are scene-envelope identity strings**, not Rust module paths and not final Reflect runtime identity. The existing rge-data fixture text and scene-module docblock already pin this convention for `Transform`; this decision extends it to the remaining three components.

Reflect-backed runtime identity (`FQ_TYPE_NAME` / hashed `TYPE_ID`) can supersede or bridge this convention later through an explicit migration. Until that bridge lands, `ComponentValue.type_id` is envelope identity by deliberate choice, not by accident.

### Q2 — Visibility on camera: **explicit `Visible`**

The camera entity will carry `Visibility::Visible` explicitly. Justification: fixture readability and schema pinning. The `Inherited` default remains correct for future entities that genuinely want to defer to a parent.

### Q3 — Camera FOV: **`fov_y_radians: 1.0471976`**

Use the `FRAC_PI_3`-compatible `f32` literal already justified by the audit (line-cited from `crates/components-render/src/camera.rs:31-39`).

### Follow-up

Exactly one bounded dispatch will be added to the brief: extend `golden-projects/simple-scene/scenes/main.rge-scene` with the four envelope payloads and extend `crates/rge-data/tests/golden_simple_scene_schema.rs` with schema-only assertions. No typed bridge, no renderer/GPU, no asset loading, no production loader changes.
